headline: SpaceX Post-Listing Collapse Threatens IPO Market’s AI Euphoria
summary: (Bloomberg) -- SpaceX’s fall from its post-listing peak to below its IPO price in just a month poured cold water on the market for newly public companies, dragging a key gauge of this year’s debuts down with it.Most Read from BloombergThailand Scraps Plan to End Visa-Free Entry for Indian TouristsBeckham’s IM8 Gets $1 Billion From General Catalyst for GrowthCIA Says AI Drones Give Russian Troops Only 30 Minutes to LiveOpenAI’s First Device Will Be Movable, Screenless Speaker Built as AI Companio

headline: Is Blackstone Inc. (BX) One of the Best Dividend Stocks to Invest in According to Jim Simons’ Renaissance Technologies?

headline: Williams Is No AI Pipe Dream. The Stock Is a Winner.
summary: Midstream operator  Williams Cos.  an energy middleman whose pipelines act as toll roads, handles roughly one-third of all the natural gas in the U.S. every day, connecting it from where it’s produced to where it’s used.  It’s one of the latest beneficiaries of artificial intelligence, as the company helps to provide some of the juice that increasingly electricity-hungry data centers require.  “Williams has articulated an expectation for potentially 10% earnings growth through the end of the decade and even beyond; eight to 10 years is sort of the time frame,” says Ben Cook, portfolio manager of the Hennessy Midstream fund, which owns the shares.

It’s one of the latest beneficiaries of artificial intelligence, as the company helps to provide some of the juice that increasingly electricity-hungry data centers require.  “Williams has articulated an expectation for potentially 10% earnings growth through the end of the decade and even beyond; eight to 10 years is sort of the time frame,” says Ben Cook, portfolio manager of the Hennessy Midstream fund, which owns the shares.
